name: Home Repair Services of Kent County
description_short: 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing income-based home repairs, accessibility modifications, and financial counseling to vulnerable Kent County homeowners since 1979.
description_long:
Home Repair Services of Kent County was established as an independent 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization in 1979 and is headquartered in Grand Rapids, Michigan. The organization focuses exclusively on serving lower-income and vulnerable homeowners throughout Kent County, with a mission centered on strengthening homeowners to build stronger communities. Their work is guided by core values of compassion, stewardship, inclusiveness, professionalism, and collaboration.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free and income-based services divided into two main categories: Repair & Access Services and Financial Stability Programs. Repair services include critical home repairs, accessibility modifications (such as access ramps and bathroom modifications), home inspections, kitchen remodeling guidance, and hands-on DIY workshops covering topics like drywall repair, tile setting, roofing, siding repair, and electrical work. Financial Stability Programs connect homeowners with housing counselors to explore foreclosure prevention resources and offer workshops to improve financial literacy. They also operate Fix-It School Classes and a DIY Remodeling Together Program.

What distinguishes Home Repair Services is their emphasis on education and self-sufficiency alongside direct repair assistance. Rather than solely providing services to homeowners, they actively teach DIY skills through structured workshops and classes, empowering residents to maintain and improve their homes independently. Their Lunch & Learn Tours offer community members deeper insight into available resources. The organization maintains professional standards in the building trades while serving a traditionally underserved population, combining skilled labor with financial counseling under one nonprofit roof.

Home Repair Services is legitimately a free-help nonprofit with HUD-aligned housing counseling services. Prospective clients should note that services are income-based and targeted to vulnerable homeowners in Kent County specifically, meaning eligibility requirements likely apply. While their website demonstrates active programming and multiple service offerings, detailed eligibility criteria, wait times, and service availability are not fully documented on their public website.
pros: Income-based services specifically designed for vulnerable and lower-income homeowners
Offers both direct repair assistance and hands-on DIY educational workshops to build homeowner self-sufficiency
Housing counselors available to help explore foreclosure prevention and financial stability options
Free educational events including Fix-It Classes, workshops on specific repairs (tile, drywall, roofing, siding, electrical), and Lunch & Learn tours
Accessibility modification services (ramps, bathroom modifications) address mobility needs often overlooked by general contractors
Established 501(c)(3) nonprofit with 45+ years of community presence and 24-person staff
Regular event calendar with monthly Lunch & Learn tours and specialized workshops throughout the year
cons: Services are geographically limited to Kent County, Michigan — not available outside this region
Website lacks clear eligibility requirements, income thresholds, or application process details
No publicly listed wait times, service timelines, or capacity information for home repair requests
Limited transparency on how to actually register for repair services versus educational workshops
Website social media integration errors prevent full engagement with followers (LinkedIn/social feeds show errors)
best_for: Lower-income homeowners in Kent County facing foreclosure who need financial counseling and stability resources
Vulnerable homeowners seeking free educational workshops to learn DIY home maintenance and repair skills
Seniors and people with mobility limitations needing affordable accessibility modifications like ramps or bathroom updates
Homeowners who want to combine professional repairs with financial literacy workshops to improve overall housing stability
diagnosis: Home Repair Services of Kent County is best for lower-income and vulnerable homeowners in Kent County, Michigan who need free or affordable home repairs, accessibility modifications, foreclosure counseling, or educational workshops to build self-sufficiency. Primary caveat: services are geographically limited to Kent County and eligibility requirements are not transparently detailed online, requiring direct contact to confirm qualification and service availability.
services: Income-based home repair services for critical structural and safety issues
Accessibility modifications including access ramps and bathroom modifications
Housing counseling for foreclosure prevention and alternatives
Financial literacy workshops and financial coaching
Free hands-on DIY workshops (drywall repair, tile setting, roofing, siding, electrical)
Fix-It School Classes on home maintenance fundamentals
Lunch & Learn presentations and facility tours
Specialized workshops (e.g., 'Handy Helen' electrical outlet replacement class)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1100 Division Ave S, Grand Rapids, MI 49507
